Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced kidney disease. Hemoglobin A1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.
